description: System-level TravelAgent renovation to standardize chat response contracts, planner stage visibility, feedback traceability, and cross-panel UI state handling.

userStories.title: Add unified conversation response DTO
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want a single response DTO for chat results so that every specialist returns a stable top-level contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Define a chat response DTO or equivalent schema with stable top-level fields for agentType, answer, travelPlan, timeline, feedbackTarget, and issues
Optional blocks serialize as explicit null or empty collections instead of omitted fields
The chat controller returns the unified DTO for successful requests
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Map all specialists to the unified response contract
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want WEATHER, GEO, GENERAL, and TRAVEL_PLANNER outputs normalized so that the frontend does not need specialist-specific parsing branches.
acceptanceCriteria: WEATHER, GEO, GENERAL, and TRAVEL_PLANNER responses are all mapped through one response builder or equivalent adapter
Missing travelPlan, timeline, feedbackTarget, and issues fields use stable empty semantics for non-applicable specialists
The HTTP layer no longer contains per-specialist response shape branching
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define normalized planner stage events
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want planner stages expressed through one event model so that execution progress is consistent across persistence and SSE.
acceptanceCriteria: Introduce a planner event model with stage, status, message, startedAt, and endedAt fields
Planner events can represent running, completed, failed, and repaired outcomes or clear equivalents
Non-planner responses expose an empty timeline collection instead of planner-specific placeholders
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Persist and stream normalized planner events
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want normalized planner events stored and streamed so that the same stage data is visible in history and live updates.
acceptanceCriteria: SSE timeline messages use the normalized planner event shape
Persisted timeline records keep stage, status, message, and timestamps from planner execution
Repair and failure paths emit distinct normalized events
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add structured missing-information summary
userStories.description: As a travel planning user, I want the system to tell me what facts are missing so that I know how to complete an incomplete request.
acceptanceCriteria: Planner responses expose a structured missingInformation list or equivalent field for required travel facts
Each missing item includes a stable category such as destination, date, budget, or companions
When required facts are missing, the final answer text stays consistent with the structured list
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add structured constraint summary
userStories.description: As a travel planning user, I want a structured summary of validation and repair outcomes so that I can judge whether a plan is ready to use.
acceptanceCriteria: Planner responses expose a constraintSummary block with a status of passed, repaired, or risk, or clear equivalents
Constraint summary includes structured issue items for validation failures or repair actions
Final answer text does not contradict the structured constraint summary
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Expose versioned feedback targets in chat results
userStories.description: As a product analyst, I want each response to expose a stable feedback target so that later feedback can be tied to the exact answer or plan version.
acceptanceCriteria: Chat responses include a stable feedback target identifier and scope for answer, plan, or overall evaluation
Planner results expose a stable plan version or equivalent revision identifier
Non-planner responses still expose a valid answer-level feedback target
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Persist feedback reason labels and version linkage
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want feedback records to store scope, version linkage, and reasons so that feedback data is analyzable after the renovation.
acceptanceCriteria: Feedback persistence stores feedback target id, scope, reason labels, and optional free-text comment
Existing feedback reads remain backward compatible or are migrated safely
Stored feedback can be joined back to the related answer or plan version
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update feedback submit and export APIs
userStories.description: As a product analyst, I want feedback APIs to accept and expose structured reasons so that acceptance and rejection patterns can be summarized reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: Feedback submit API accepts structured reason labels for partially_accept and reject outcomes
Accept flow remains supported without requiring a reason label
Feedback export or summary output includes target id, scope, and reason labels
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create a shared frontend conversation result adapter
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want one adapter for chat responses so that all panels consume the same normalized view model.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a shared frontend adapter or view model layer for the unified chat response contract
The adapter derives panel state values for loading, partial, success, empty, and error
Core panels can read normalized answer, travelPlan, timeline, feedback target, and issue fields from the shared adapter
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor the chat result card to use the shared view model
userStories.description: As a user, I want the chat result card to show answer status, missing facts, and plan risk consistently so that incomplete results are understandable.
acceptanceCriteria: The chat result card reads normalized answer, missing information, and constraint summary data from the shared view model
Incomplete planner responses render a visible partial state instead of a generic success state
The card shows clear status copy for empty and error cases
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor the itinerary panel to use the shared view model
userStories.description: As a user, I want the itinerary panel to stay in sync with the chat answer so that plan details never disagree across panels.
acceptanceCriteria: The itinerary panel reads travelPlan and related status data from the shared view model
When travelPlan is absent, the panel renders empty or partial state instead of a generic error
Destination and date summary shown in the itinerary panel matches the chat answer for the same response
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor the map panel to use the shared view model
userStories.description: As a user, I want the map panel to explain when map data is unavailable so that partial planner results do not look broken.
acceptanceCriteria: The map panel reads normalized locations or map state from the shared view model
Missing map data renders an empty or partial explanation instead of a silent blank panel
Map content shown for a successful planner response matches the same response's itinerary context
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor the feedback panel for versioned targets and reason tags
userStories.description: As a user, I want feedback actions tied to the exact response version so that my accept or reject action is recorded against the correct result.
acceptanceCriteria: Feedback UI submits the normalized feedback target id and scope from the shared view model
Partially Accept and Reject allow selecting structured reason tags and adding an optional comment
Accept remains a one-click action for the current response
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add shared cross-panel state rendering
userStories.description: As a user, I want all major panels to use the same status patterns so that loading, partial completion, and errors are predictable everywhere.
acceptanceCriteria: Chat, itinerary, map, and feedback panels all render loading, partial, success, empty, and error states
SSE interruption or API failure surfaces a recovery action without requiring a full page refresh
Shared status copy or shared status components are used across the affected panels
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add an execution timeline panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want to inspect the execution timeline so that I can understand when the system generated, validated, repaired, or failed a plan.
acceptanceCriteria: The UI shows planner timeline entries with stage, status, timestamp, and message
Repair, failure, and tool-provider related events are visually distinguishable in the timeline
The timeline remains readable on desktop and mobile layouts
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add backend regression tests for the Herness contract
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want automated backend regression coverage for the renovated contract so that future changes do not silently break core conversation flows.
acceptanceCriteria: Add backend regression coverage for representative planner, weather, geo, and feedback flows
Regression tests assert the unified response contract, normalized timeline shape, and error-path behavior
Standard backend test commands execute the new coverage
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add frontend regression tests for shared view model states
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want automated frontend regression coverage for the shared view model so that panel state behavior stays consistent after future UI changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Add frontend tests for shared view model derivation logic
Add UI tests for loading, partial, success, empty, and error rendering across the affected panels
Standard frontend test commands execute the new coverage
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update architecture and development documentation
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the renovation contract and validation commands documented so that implementation details and operating instructions stay aligned.
acceptanceCriteria: Update architecture or development docs to describe the unified response contract, timeline semantics, and feedback linkage fields
Document the backend and frontend regression commands needed for the renovated workflow
Docs reference the finalized Herness branch or feature name consistently
Typecheck passes
